bulk_import.upload_a_csv_or_tsv_file_fro.original:
Upload a CSV or TSV file from Excel, Google Sheets, Notion, or any inventory tool.
        Column names are auto-detected. No AI required — fastest import method.
bulk_import.upload_a_csv_or_tsv_file_fro.originalHtml: Upload a CSV or TSV file from Excel, Google Sheets, Notion, or any inventory tool. Column names are auto-detected. No AI required — fastest import method.

bulk_import.create_a_zip_where_each_subf.original:
Create a ZIP where each subfolder contains photos of one component.
        Upload it here — the AI identifies each component from its photos.
        Matches the existing folder-import workflow.
bulk_import.create_a_zip_where_each_subf.originalHtml: Create a ZIP where each subfolder contains photos of one component. Upload it here — the AI identifies each component from its photos. Matches the existing folder-import workflow.

bulk_import.drag_photos_directly_into_yo.original:
Drag photos directly into your browser and group them by component.
        No folder structure needed — just drop your photos and let AI do the rest.
        Best for ad-hoc imports straight from a camera roll.

bulk_import_zip.images_directly_in_the_r.original:
💡  (images directly in the root) = one component named after the ZIP file.
       (one folder per component) = multiple components imported at once.
       is optional but improves AI accuracy. Lines like:
bulk_import_zip.images_directly_in_the_r.originalHtml:
💡 Flat ZIP (images directly in the root) = one component named after the ZIP file.
Subfolder ZIP (one folder per component) = multiple components imported at once.
description.txt is optional but improves AI accuracy. Lines like: Product Name: ESP32-C3 | Category: Microcontroller
